channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Chicago spent $80K to renovate office for first lady Johnson as city faces billion dollar budget shortfall
item.description: Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson is facing scrutiny after the city spent $80,000 to renovate an office for first lady Stacie Johnson while facing a nearly billion-dollar budget shortfall.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Data shows migrants aren’t taking jobs from Black or Hispanic people, despite what Trump says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump promises the biggest deportation event the U.S. has ever seen if he is elected — a promise he has predicated, in part, on the notion that immigrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump, Kamala Harris still neck-and-neck in swing states
item.description: Data coming out of critical swing states show that any polling margins remain razor thin and within the margin of error
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Doctor deems Harris in 'excellent health.' Her team aims to contrast with Trump's scant medical info
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris' doctor says she is in “excellent health” and “possesses the physical and mental resiliency” to serve as president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ted Cruz and Colin Allred wage another big US Senate fight in Texas
item.description: Republicans have a stranglehold on every statewide elected office in Texas and control the state Supreme Court, both chambers of the Legislature and both U.S. Senate seats.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy And Juan A. Lozano, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: No Dem effort to stymie Trump election win certification: Strategists
item.description: A few Democratic lawmakers say they will accept a free and fair Trump election win, but aren't sure he'll be fair or honest.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: If you let your flood insurance lapse and then got hit by Helene, you may be able to renew it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Residents in the states hit by Hurricane Helene who had coverage through the federal flood insurance program but let it lapse before the storm hit may be able to renew it and still be covered from the impact.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson discusses government transparency, public safety, and housing
item.description: Henderson City Council candidate Dr. Monica Larson joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Friday to discuss issues she believes need to be addressed in the city.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Are male voters reluctant to vote for a woman? Harris' backers are confronting the question head on
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The concern has been there all along, but now it's being talked about openly: Are some men reluctant to vote for Democrat Kamala Harris because she’s a woman? The vice president rarely references her gender on the campaign trail, but her key supporters are starting to make more dir
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: 'Brothers' for Kamala
item.description: The latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doug Emhoff doesn't deny report he slapped ex-girlfriend outside overseas movie event
item.description: Second gentleman Doug Emhoff called salacious allegations against him a "distraction" after a Daily Mail report claimed he once struck an ex-girlfriend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris viewed more positively by Hispanic women than by Hispanic men: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A solid majority of Hispanic women have a positive opinion of Vice President Kamala Harris and a negative view of former President Donald Trump, but Hispanic men are more divided on both candidates, according to a recent poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk unveils Tesla's 'Cybercab,' plans to bring autonomous driving tech to other models in 2025
item.description: Tesla unveiled its long-awaited robotaxi at a Hollywood studio Thursday night, though fans of the electric vehicle maker will have to wait until at least 2026 before they are available. CEO Elon Musk said the “Cybercab” doesn’t have steering wheels or pedals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate candidates steer clear of debates
item.description: Senate candidates this cycle are largely steering clear of the debate stage, in some cases avoiding it altogether, marking a major change from past years as campaigns question the debates' importance. For years, it was common for top Senate races to feature multiple debates.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who could replace interim NYPD Commissioner Donlon if he resigns?
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon is expected to resign following a string of high-profile exits from City Hall over the last month, sources told PIX11 News on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Annette Dawson Owens discusses education, healthcare, and affordable housing
item.description: Annette Dawson Owens, a Republican candidate for Nevada’s Assembly District 29, recently discussed her views on education, healthcare, and affordable housing on 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A federal judge rejects a call to reopen voter registration in Georgia after Hurricane Helene
item.description: A federal judge said Thursday that she won't order the presidential battleground state of Georgia to reopen voter registration for November’s elections despite recent disruptions to registration caused by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NJ assemblyman's car stolen, house broken into
item.description: HAZLET, N.J. (PIX11) - Video from the home where a New Jersey assemblyman is currently living shows alleged burglars break into the home and speed away with his personal car - complete with State Assembly plates.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Arizona Senate debate
item.description: Rep. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) and Republican candidate Kari Lake faced off in their only debate in the Arizona Senate race Wednesday, and the two contenders frequently traded barbs. Gallego and Lake, who are vying for retiring Sen.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Trump started selling the "God Bless the USA" Bibles in partnership with country musician Lee Greenwood earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Trump secretly sent Putin COVID-19 tests during pandemic shortage, a new book reports
item.description: Vice President Harris is criticizing Trump over the reported shipment, which is detailed in a new book by the journalist Bob Woodward. The president's campaign has denied the reporting.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Harris, Walz appeared on ABC's 'The View' and 'Jimmy Kimmel Live'
item.description: The Democratic presidential ticket is on a media blitz in New York. Vice President Kamala Harris appeared Tuesday on ABC's “The View," and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz appeared on ABC's “Jimmy Kimmel Live” on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Rafael Arroyo discusses wasteful spending, studio tax credits, and California's policies in Nevada
item.description: Rafael Arroyo, the Republican candidate running for Assembly District 41, joined Brian Loftis on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide, outlining his vision and priorities as the election approaches.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michael Naft discusses traffic safety, F1, and animal welfare in the Las Vegas valley
item.description: Michael Naft, incumbent county commissioner for District 8, joined Brian Loftis on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Monday, discussing his campaign priorities as election day nears.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: It’s a close race for Michigan. These factors could decide it for Harris or Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris needs to win back Michigan voters President Joe Biden looked poised to lose. Donald Trump’s appeal remains strong here, but he’s not without his own struggles.
item.dc:creator: Majd Al-Waheidi, MPR News
